data.title: dogwifhat (WIF) price poised for $0.36 bounce, trading at $0.34 now. Technicals hint at 6% gain in a week.
data.description: The cryptocurrency Dogwifhat (WIF) is currently priced at $0.34 per token. Technical analysis indicates a possible 6% increase to reach the $0.36 resistance level in the upcoming week. The short-term target for WIF within the next week is expected to see a rise to $0.36.

data.title: ALGO projected to reach $0.16-$0.19 by Feb 2026. Short-term target: $0.13-$0.14, bullish breakout at $0.14, critical support at $0.11.

data.title: Robert Half recognized by Fortune as one of the World's Most Admired Companies for 29th consecutive year. $RHI.
data.description: A prestigious global talent solutions and business consulting firm, Robert Half, based in Menlo Park, California, along with its subsidiary Protiviti®, has earned the noteworthy recognition of being named one of the 2026 World's Most Admired Companies by Fortune. This achievement highlights Robert Half's position as part of an exclusive group of esteemed companies recognized for their excellence in various business sectors. The firm, listed on the NYSE under the ticker symbol RHI, continues to demonstrate its commitment to exceptional service and industry leadership.

data.title: BGL arranged $135M in financing for Hall of Fame Village waterpark and hotel projects in Cleveland.
data.description: $135 million in real estate financing has been structured by Brown Gibbons Lang & Company (BGL) to progress waterpark and hotel projects. BGL, a prominent independent investment bank, revealed this development on Jan. 22, 2026.

data.title: ADM (NYSE: ADM) named one of world's most admired food production companies in FORTUNE 2026 list. Developed by FORTUNE and Korn Ferry, from 1,500 candidates.
data.description: ADM (NYSE: ADM) has been recognized as one of the top companies in the food production industry for 2026 by FORTUNE Magazine. This prestigious accolade places ADM among the world's most admired companies in the sector. The list, compiled by FORTUNE in collaboration with Korn Ferry, is a comprehensive assessment of corporate reputation. The selection process involved evaluating approximately 1,500 candidates, including the 1,000 largest U.S. companies based on revenue, as well as international firms listed in the Fortune Global 500 database.

data.title: Alpenrose closing manufacturing facility in Clackamas, OR, to consolidate ops; Fresh milk production continues in Kent, Wash.
data.description: Alpenrose has announced plans to consolidate operations by shutting down its butter, sour cream, and ice cream mix manufacturing plant in Clackamas, OR. This decision, effective from March 31, 2026, will impact production at the facility. However, the production of fresh milk will carry on at the Pacific Northwest facility in Kent, Wash. This move ensures that Alpenrose's popular milk and cottage cheese products will still be accessible in local grocery stores throughout the region. To assist in this transition, Alpenrose has enlisted the services of Harry Davis & Company.

data.title: CEO Paul Markovich urges Congress to address healthcare system failures causing high costs and lack of coverage for Americans.
data.description: On January 22, 2026, it was reported that the president and CEO of Ascendiun, the parent company of Blue Shield of California, Paul Markovich, highlighted the serious issues in the healthcare system. Markovich emphasized that the systemic failures have led to exorbitant costs and a large number of Americans lacking access to affordable insurance. This revelation underscores the critical need for reform and improvement within the healthcare sector to address the growing concerns faced by individuals and families across the country.

data.title: BrainTap had 2.2M sessions and 46.6M minutes listened in 2025. New ski-focused series ignites 2026.
data.description: Kicking off 2026 with a fresh ski-focused series is Dr. Tom Lankering in New Bern, North Carolina. BrainTap, a top player in audio-guided brainwave entrainment and mental wellness tech, recently unveiled their 2025 year-end figures. The report highlights extraordinary achievements and impressive progress, hinting at a record-breaking performance.

data.title: Finseca praises FSI for progress in financial security profession. SEC's no-action letter supports ensemble payments.
data.description: In January 2026, it was reported that after ten years of efforts by the Financial Services Institute (FSI), the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) granted a no-action letter endorsing ensemble payments. This new development permits companies to issue payments to entities rather than individuals. The FSI's persistent work culminated in this milestone, which marks a significant shift in payment practices within the financial industry.

data.title: Bitwise introduces actively managed ETF pairing Bitcoin and gold, blending assets to diversify portfolios.
data.description: A newly managed fund has combined Bitcoin, precious metals, and mining stocks in its portfolio as part of efforts by asset managers to increase the presence of cryptocurrency in broader macroeconomic and capital-preservation strategies. This fund is actively managed and aims to leverage the potential of these assets for greater returns and diversification. The move signifies a growing trend in the financial industry to incorporate digital assets like Bitcoin alongside traditional investment options. This strategy reflects the current market sentiment towards alternative assets and the increasing acceptance of cryptocurrency as a viable investment.

data.title: OHA provides financing for Berkshire's acquisition of UFT, a top distributor in water treatment sectors, on Jan. 22, 2026.
data.description: Oak Hill Advisors (OHA) recently acted as Joint Lead Arranger in providing financial support for Berkshire Partners' acquisition of United Flow Technologies (UFT), a prominent technical distributor and solutions provider specializing in the municipal and industrial water and wastewater treatment industries. The acquisition marks a significant move in the sector, with UFT holding a strong position in these markets.

data.title: Mississippi bill aims to remove financial barriers for genetic testing, screenings endorsed by Susan G. Komen®. Rep. Samuel Creekmore introduced the legislation.
data.description: In Jackson, Mississippi, Susan G. Komen® praises Representative Samuel Creekmore, IV (R-New Albany) for proposing a bill to remove financial obstacles for patients needing necessary genetic testing and recommended screenings. The legislation aims to provide access to germline testing, a crucial type of test that identifies inherited mutations in every cell from birth. This initiative seeks to ensure that patients receive appropriate care without financial burden.

data.title: Dogecoin's first SEC-approved ETF, TDOG, backed by Dogecoin Foundation, launched, offering exposure to meme-born cryptocurrency.
data.description: A new development in the financial world sees Dogecoin (CRYPTO: DOGE) entering the mainstream with the launch of a spot ETF supported by the Dogecoin Foundation. The 21shares Dogecoin ETF (NASDAQ: TDOG) started trading on Thursday, providing investors with a way to invest in the meme-based cryptocurrency through regular brokerage accounts. This particular ETF is the sole spot Dogecoin ETF approved by the Dogecoin Foundation, a nonprofit organization that has been backing the decentralized growth of the token since 2014. The endorsement from the Dogecoin Foundation is facilitated by House of Doge.

data.title: Saalex Corp. attains CMMC Level 2 certification for cybersecurity controls to safeguard data.
data.description: Recent independent evaluation confirms the effectiveness of Saalex Corporation's cybersecurity measures in protecting Controlled Unclassified Information. Saalex, a prominent provider of engineering, IT, and technical services in aerospace, defense, and federal industries, received validation on Jan. 22, 2026. This assessment reinforces Saalex's commitment to maintaining strong cybersecurity controls.

data.title: Lease End introduces Payoff Intelligence AI agent in Burley, Idaho & Salt Lake City to save drivers millions of hours fighting 'dark patterns'.
data.description: In Burley, Idaho, and Salt Lake City, a new technology has been introduced by Lease End called Payoff Intelligence. This AI agent is designed to combat deceptive practices known as 'dark patterns' in order to ensure accurate auto lease payoff amounts. The innovative tool is projected to assist drivers in saving a significant amount of time, estimated to be in the millions of hours.

data.title: USDC on Algorand can now be used by Kraken customers for deposits and withdrawals, offering fast, low-cost transactions.
data.description: Expansion of access to quick, cost-effective digital dollar transactions on Algorand has been facilitated through integration. As of January 22, 2026, Kraken customers can utilize USDC on Algorand for deposits and withdrawals. This integration allows for seamless transactions of USDC on the Algorand blockchain through the Kraken exchange.
